intro: Arsenjevia rossii, also known as Russian artemisia, is a perennial plant species native to the Russian Far East and northeastern China, characterized by its small, white or pale-yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Arsenjevia rossii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arsenjevia rossii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and respiratory issues.
